findings.title: Pool-directed transferFrom can burn more tokens than the approved allowance

best_agent_match.title: Pool-directed transferFrom burns more tokens than the approved allowance

findings.title: Pool-directed transfers charge the burn on top of `amount`, causing exact-balance sells and exact-amount integrations to revert

best_agent_match.title: Sell transfers require extra balance beyond the requested amount and can revert exact-balance exits

findings.title: Sell-path Transfer events do not match actual balance changes

best_agent_match.title: Sell-path Transfer events are fabricated and do not match actual balance changes

findings.title: Owner can retarget the broken sell-path logic to any arbitrary destination address

best_agent_match.title: Owner can retarget the punitive pool logic to any arbitrary destination address at any time

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can arbitrarily set pool address enabling fund theft
rejected_candidates.reason: The code does let the owner retarget the special transfer path, but it does not call external contracts during transfer, so the claimed malicious callback/reentrancy theft path is unsupported. This is better framed as owner-retargetable griefing/integration breakage, which is captured in F-004.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can arbitrarily redirect marketing fee to any address
rejected_candidates.reason: `marketingWalletAddress` is never credited in storage. On sell-path transfers the contract only emits a synthetic `Transfer` event to that address, so changing it does not redirect actual token balances or create a real fee-theft vector.

rejected_candidates.title: No timelock on critical administrative functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Lack of a timelock is a governance/centralization concern, not a standalone vulnerability here. The reportable issue is the underlying owner-retargetable broken transfer logic, not the absence of a delay mechanism.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing events for critical parameter changes
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a transparency issue only. It does not by itself cause realistic fund loss, lockup, insolvency, economic manipulation, or protocol-level DoS.

rejected_candidates.title: High owner concentration - single point of failure
rejected_candidates.reason: Generic centralization risk without a distinct exploit path beyond the specific owner-controlled behavior already captured in F-004.
